name: Taipei
summary: Taipei stands out as one of Asia's safest capitals, with exceptionally low crime rates and excellent infrastructure. The city maintains a Level 1 US State Department travel advisory with only natural disasters like earthquakes and typhoons posing moderate risks.
quickVerdict: Taipei is remarkably safe for tourists, consistently ranking among the world's safest cities with a crime rate of just 19.86. Taiwan maintains a Level 1 US travel advisory, and visitors can walk confidently day or night. While petty crime exists in tourist areas, violent crime is extremely rare. The main safety considerations are natural hazards like earthquakes and typhoons, for which Taiwan has excellent emergency systems and infrastructure.

neighborhoods.name: Xinyi District
neighborhoods.description: The upscale business district around Taipei 101 with heavy security, excellent lighting, and constant police presence. Popular with tourists and international business travelers.

neighborhoods.name: Zhongshan District
neighborhoods.description: Trendy area with excellent cafes, boutique shopping, and nightlife. Well-patrolled with good lighting and frequented by young professionals and expats.

neighborhoods.name: Da'an District
neighborhoods.description: Residential and cultural hub with excellent public transport connections. Popular Linjiang Night Market area remains busy and safe well into the night.

neighborhoods.name: Shilin District
neighborhoods.description: Home to the famous night market which draws huge crowds. Generally very safe but watch for pickpockets in crowded market areas during peak hours.

neighborhoods.name: Ximending
neighborhoods.description: The entertainment and shopping district popular with young people. Generally safe but can get crowded and chaotic, especially on weekends. Minor scams occasionally reported.

neighborhoods.name: Wanhua District
neighborhoods.description: Traditional district including older areas that can feel less safe at night. Some drug-related activity reported, though violent crime remains rare. Exercise normal caution.

scams.name: Fake Tea Shop Scam
scams.description: Vendors selling low-quality tea leaves as premium brands, especially targeting tourists unfamiliar with authentic Taiwanese tea.
scams.howToAvoid: Buy tea only from reputable shops, taste before purchasing, and research authentic tea brands beforehand.

scams.name: Overpriced Fruit Cutting
scams.description: Fruit vendors cutting samples for tourists then charging exorbitant prices up to 1000 NTD. Once cut, tourists feel obligated to pay.
scams.howToAvoid: Never ask to test fruit freshness, agree on prices before any cutting, buy whole fruits from markets instead.

scams.name: Taxi Meter Scams
scams.description: Some taxi drivers refuse to use meters and quote inflated fixed prices, especially at airports or tourist areas at night.
scams.howToAvoid: Insist on meter use saying 'Yòng biǎo' in Mandarin, use ride-hailing apps like Uber, or use official taxi stands at airports.

scams.name: Counterfeit Attraction Tickets
scams.description: Street sellers offering discounted tickets to Taipei 101 observatory and other attractions that turn out to be fake.
scams.howToAvoid: Purchase tickets only from official venues, authorized partners, or verified online platforms like Klook.

soloFemale.overview: Taipei is exceptionally safe for solo female travelers, ranking among the world's safest cities. Women regularly travel alone day and night with minimal concerns. The MRT features women-only waiting areas during late hours, and locals are helpful and respectful. Taiwan's progressive stance including Asia's first same-sex marriage legalization contributes to a generally safe environment for all travelers.
tips: Take advantage of women-only waiting areas on MRT platforms marked after dark
Solo dining at cafes and restaurants is completely normal and widely accepted
Use well-lit main streets rather than dark alleys late at night, though serious crime is rare
Carry a translation app as English isn't widely spoken outside tourist areas
Women traveling alone should avoid excessive displays of wealth in crowded night markets

nightSafety.overview: Taipei maintains excellent nighttime safety with well-lit streets, 24/7 convenience stores every few blocks, and active night markets until midnight. The city has extensive CCTV coverage and police presence. Night markets like Shilin and Raohe remain busy with families and tourists past midnight, creating natural safety through numbers.
tips: Night markets are generally the safest nighttime activities with constant crowds and security
MRT runs until midnight with good security coverage and emergency phones
Stick to main streets and well-lit areas rather than narrow alleys
24/7 convenience stores provide safe refuge and assistance points throughout the city
Avoid confrontational behavior as 'losing face' publicly can escalate situations unnecessarily

transport.metro: The Taipei MRT is exceptionally safe, clean, and efficient with bilingual signage and announcements. It operates 6AM to midnight with frequent service and excellent security coverage. Women-only waiting areas are available on platforms during late hours.
transport.rideshare: Uber operates legally in Taiwan through partnerships with licensed taxi fleets. Both Uber and local apps like 55688 Taxi provide safe alternatives with tracking and driver verification. Fares are reasonable at NT$100-200 for short city rides.
transport.taxis: Licensed taxis are generally safe and affordable. Insist on meter use or agree on fares beforehand. Female travelers should exercise caution taking taxis alone at night, though serious incidents are rare. Most drivers have limited English but are honest.
transport.tips: The EasyCard works across all transport modes and provides discounts. Public transport is significantly safer and more efficient than driving in dense city traffic.

customs: Remove shoes when entering homes and some traditional establishments
Avoid sticking chopsticks upright in rice bowls as this resembles incense for the dead
Give gifts with both hands and don't open them immediately in front of the giver
Keep voices down on public transport and queue orderly without cutting in line
Respect the concept of 'face' by avoiding public criticism or embarrassment of others

health.overview: Taiwan has world-class healthcare with many hospitals holding international accreditation. The National Health Insurance system ensures high-quality, cost-effective care. Medical tourism is popular due to excellent facilities and advanced technology.
health.water: Tap water is safe to drink in Taipei, though many locals prefer filtered or bottled water. Water quality meets international standards.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required. Standard travel vaccines like MMR are recommended. Taiwan has excellent disease surveillance and control.
health.altitude: Taipei sits near sea level with subtropical climate. Summers are hot and humid, winters mild. Typhoon season runs May-November with advance warning systems.

emergency.usEmbassy: American Institute in Taiwan - Taipei: +886-2-2162-2000, No. 100 Jinhu Road, Neihu District, Taipei 114017. 24-hour emergency line available.

faq.a: Yes, Taipei is exceptionally safe for tourists with one of the world's lowest crime rates at 19.86. Taiwan maintains a US Level 1 travel advisory and visitors can walk confidently day and night with minimal safety concerns.

Taipei is very safe at night with excellent street lighting, active night markets until midnight, and 24/7 convenience stores every few blocks. The MRT runs until midnight and police presence is strong throughout the city.

faq.q: Is Taipei safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Taipei is extremely safe for solo female travelers and ranks among the world's safest cities for women. The MRT has women-only waiting areas at night, and solo dining and travel are completely normal and accepted.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Taipei?
faq.a: Taipei has no dangerous no-go areas, but exercise normal caution in parts of Wanhua District at night. All major tourist areas including night markets, Xinyi, Ximending, and transport hubs are very safe.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Taipei?
faq.a: Yes, Taipei's public transport is exceptionally safe, clean, and efficient. The MRT has excellent security, CCTV coverage, and bilingual announcements. It's significantly safer than driving and the preferred way to get around the city.
